words.meaning_en: an advantage or profit gained from something
words.derivation: bene(好的) + fit(做) → 做好事 → 益处
words.example: Exercise has many health benefits.
words.example_zh: 运动有很多健康方面的益处。

words.breakdown: bene-(好的) + fic-(做) + -ial(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: favorable or advantageous; resulting in good
words.derivation: bene(好的) + fic(做) + ial(…的) → 做好事的 → 有益的
words.example: A good diet is beneficial to health.
words.example_zh: 良好的饮食对健康有益。

words.breakdown: bene-(好的) + vol-(意愿) + -ent(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: well-meaning and kindly
words.derivation: bene(好的) + vol(意愿) + ent(…的) → 有好意愿的 → 仁慈的
words.example: The benevolent organization provided food for the homeless.
words.example_zh: 这个慈善组织为无家可归者提供食物。

words.meaning_en: a person who gives money or other help to a person or cause
words.derivation: bene(好的) + fact(做) + or(人) → 做好事的人 → 恩人
words.example: The museum was built thanks to a generous benefactor.
words.example_zh: 这座博物馆的建成多亏了一位慷慨的捐助者。

words.meaning_en: the utterance or bestowing of a blessing
words.derivation: bene(好的) + dict(说) + ion(行为) → 说好话 → 祝福
words.example: The priest offered a benediction at the end of the ceremony.
words.example_zh: 牧师在仪式结束时献上了祝福。

words.meaning_en: a failure to function in a normal or satisfactory manner
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + function(功能) → 功能变坏 → 故障
words.example: A malfunction in the engine caused the plane to land early.
words.example_zh: 发动机故障导致飞机提前降落。

words.meaning_en: intending or intended to do harm
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + icious(充满…的) → 充满坏心的 → 恶意的
words.example: The malicious software damaged thousands of computers.
words.example_zh: 这个恶意软件损坏了数千台电脑。

words.meaning_en: lack of proper nutrition
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + nutrition(营养) → 营养状况不好 → 营养不良
words.example: Malnutrition is a serious problem in developing countries.
words.example_zh: 营养不良是发展中国家的一个严重问题。

words.meaning_en: very virulent or infectious; tending to produce death
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + ignant(具有…性质的) → 具有恶性的 → 恶性的
words.example: The doctor discovered a malignant tumor in the patient.
words.example_zh: 医生在患者体内发现了一个恶性肿瘤。

words.meaning_en: improper, illegal, or negligent professional activity
words.derivation: mal(坏的) + practice(实践) → 不良的执业行为 → 渎职
words.example: The doctor was sued for medical malpractice.
words.example_zh: 这位医生因医疗事故被起诉。

words.meaning_en: to say or estimate that something will happen in the future
words.derivation: pre(提前) + dict(说) → 提前说出 → 预测
words.example: Scientists predict that sea levels will continue to rise.
words.example_zh: 科学家预测海平面将继续上升。

words.meaning_en: to deny the truth of a statement by asserting the opposite
words.derivation: contra(相反) + dict(说) → 反着说 → 反驳
words.example: The new evidence contradicts the previous theory.
words.example_zh: 新证据与之前的理论相矛盾。

words.meaning_en: to state or order authoritatively; to control or influence
words.derivation: dict(说) + ate(做) → 说了就要做 → 命令
words.example: The teacher dictated the notes to the students.
words.example_zh: 老师向学生口述了笔记。

words.meaning_en: a sign or piece of information that indicates something
words.derivation: in(朝向) + dict(说) + ation(行为) → 朝着你说的东西 → 指示
words.example: There is no indication that the economy will improve soon.
words.example_zh: 没有迹象表明经济将很快好转。

words.meaning_en: a decision on a disputed issue in a civil or criminal case
words.derivation: ver(真实) + dict(说) → 说出真相 → 裁决
words.example: The jury reached a verdict of not guilty.
words.example_zh: 陪审团做出了无罪裁决。

words.meaning_en: to give an account in words of someone or something
words.derivation: de(完全) + scrib(写) → 完全写下来 → 描述
words.example: Can you describe the painting you saw at the museum?
words.example_zh: 你能描述一下你在博物馆看到的那幅画吗？

words.meaning_en: to advise and authorize the use of a medicine or treatment
words.derivation: pre(提前) + scrib(写) → 提前写好 → 开处方
words.example: The doctor prescribed antibiotics for the infection.
words.example_zh: 医生为感染开了抗生素处方。

words.meaning_en: to arrange to receive something regularly; to express agreement
words.derivation: sub(在下方) + scrib(写) → 在下方签名 → 订阅
words.example: Many students subscribe to academic journals online.
words.example_zh: 许多学生在线订阅学术期刊。

words.meaning_en: a book, document, or piece of music written by hand
words.derivation: manu(手) + script(写) → 用手写的 → 手稿
words.example: The ancient manuscript was discovered in a cave.
words.example_zh: 这份古代手稿是在一个洞穴中发现的。

words.meaning_en: words inscribed, as on a monument or in a book
words.derivation: in(在…上) + script(写) + ion(行为) → 刻写在上面 → 铭文
words.example: The inscription on the tomb dates back to 300 BC.
words.example_zh: 墓碑上的铭文可以追溯到公元前300年。

words.meaning_en: to carry or convey from one place to another
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + port(搬运) → 跨越搬运 → 运输
words.example: Trucks transport goods across the country.
words.example_zh: 卡车在全国各地运输货物。

words.meaning_en: to send goods or services to another country for sale
words.derivation: ex(向外) + port(运送) → 向外运送 → 出口
words.example: The country exports large quantities of oil.
words.example_zh: 该国出口大量石油。

words.meaning_en: to bring goods or services into a country from abroad
words.derivation: im(向内) + port(运送) → 向内运送 → 进口
words.example: Many countries import raw materials for manufacturing.
words.example_zh: 许多国家进口原材料用于制造业。

words.meaning_en: able to be easily carried or moved
words.derivation: port(携带) + able(能…的) → 能携带的 → 便携式的
words.example: She bought a portable computer for traveling.
words.example_zh: 她买了一台便携式电脑用于旅行。

words.meaning_en: to give a spoken or written account of something
words.derivation: re(回) + port(携带) → 带回消息 → 报告
words.example: The journalist will report on the latest developments.
words.example_zh: 记者将报道最新进展。

words.meaning_en: to organize and carry out; to lead or guide
words.derivation: con(共同) + duct(引导) → 一起引导 → 实施
words.example: The professor will conduct a series of experiments.
words.example_zh: 教授将进行一系列实验。

words.meaning_en: to bring something into use or operation for the first time
words.derivation: intro(向内) + duc(引导) → 引导进来 → 介绍
words.example: The company introduced a new product line last year.
words.example_zh: 该公司去年推出了新的产品线。

words.meaning_en: to make or manufacture from components or raw materials
words.derivation: pro(向前) + duc(引导) → 向前引出 → 生产
words.example: This factory produces electronic components.
words.example_zh: 这家工厂生产电子元件。

words.meaning_en: to make smaller or less in amount, degree, or size
words.derivation: re(回) + duc(引导) → 引导回去 → 减少
words.example: We need to reduce our carbon emissions.
words.example_zh: 我们需要减少碳排放。

words.meaning_en: to succeed in persuading or influencing someone to do something
words.derivation: in(向内) + duc(引导) → 引导进入某种状态 → 诱导
words.example: Stress can induce a variety of health problems.
words.example_zh: 压力可以引发各种健康问题。

words.meaning_en: to look at something closely in order to assess its condition
words.derivation: in(向内) + spect(看) → 往里面看 → 检查
words.example: Officials will inspect the building for safety violations.
words.example_zh: 官员将检查建筑物是否存在安全违规。

words.breakdown: per-(通过) + spect-(看) + -ive(名词/形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a particular attitude toward or way of regarding something
words.derivation: per(通过) + spect(看) + ive(…的) → 通过某种方式去看 → 视角
words.example: Try to see the issue from a different perspective.
words.example_zh: 试着从不同的角度来看待这个问题。

words.meaning_en: a band of colors or a range of related values or qualities
words.derivation: spect(看) + rum(名词后缀) → 所看到的色带 → 光谱
words.example: The political spectrum ranges from left to right.
words.example_zh: 政治光谱从左到右分布。

words.meaning_en: an individual animal, plant, or piece of a mineral used as an example
words.derivation: spec(看) + imen(名词后缀) → 拿来看的东西 → 标本
words.example: The biologist collected a rare specimen in the forest.
words.example_zh: 生物学家在森林里采集了一个稀有标本。

words.meaning_en: a person who watches an event, especially a sports event
words.derivation: spect(看) + ator(…的人) → 看的人 → 观众
words.example: Thousands of spectators gathered to watch the game.
words.example_zh: 数千名观众聚集在一起观看比赛。

words.meaning_en: to change the form, character, or function of something
words.derivation: con(完全) + vert(转) → 完全转变 → 转换
words.example: The factory was converted into a museum.
words.example_zh: 这家工厂被改建成了博物馆。

words.meaning_en: showing a great deal of variety; very different
words.derivation: di(分开) + vers(转) → 向不同方向转 → 多样的
words.example: New York City has a diverse population.
words.example_zh: 纽约市拥有多元化的人口。

words.meaning_en: to move backward; to change to the opposite direction
words.derivation: re(回) + vers(转) → 转回去 → 反转
words.example: The court decided to reverse the previous ruling.
words.example_zh: 法院决定推翻先前的裁决。

words.breakdown: contra-(相反) + vers-(转) + -ial(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: giving rise or likely to give rise to public disagreement
words.derivation: contra(相反) + vers(转) + ial(…的) → 转向相反方向的 → 有争议的
words.example: The new policy is highly controversial among citizens.
words.example_zh: 新政策在市民中极具争议。

words.meaning_en: able to adapt or be adapted to many different functions
words.derivation: vers(转) + atile(能…的) → 能灵活转变的 → 多才多艺的
words.example: She is a versatile musician who plays several instruments.
words.example_zh: 她是一位多才多艺的音乐家，会演奏多种乐器。

words.meaning_en: to cause something to arise or come about
words.derivation: gen(产生) + er + ate(使) → 使之产生 → 生成
words.example: Wind turbines generate electricity from wind.
words.example_zh: 风力涡轮机利用风力发电。

words.meaning_en: all of the people born and living at about the same time
words.derivation: gen(出生) + er + ation(行为) → 一起出生的 → 一代人
words.example: The younger generation is more comfortable with technology.
words.example_zh: 年轻一代对科技更加适应。

words.meaning_en: a unit of heredity transferred from a parent to offspring
words.derivation: gen(产生) → 产生生命特征的单位 → 基因
words.example: Scientists have identified the gene responsible for this disease.
words.example_zh: 科学家已经确定了导致这种疾病的基因。

words.meaning_en: relating to genes or heredity
words.derivation: gen(起源) + etic(…的) → 与起源相关的 → 遗传的
words.example: Genetic research has advanced significantly in recent years.
words.example_zh: 近年来基因研究取得了重大进展。

words.breakdown: indi-(在…之内) + gen-(出生) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: originating or occurring naturally in a particular place; native
words.derivation: indi(在…之内) + gen(出生) + ous(…的) → 在当地出生的 → 本土的
words.example: These plants are indigenous to South America.
words.example_zh: 这些植物原产于南美洲。

words.breakdown: sym-(共同) + path-(感情) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: feelings of pity and sorrow for someone else's misfortune
words.derivation: sym(共同) + path(感情) + y(名词后缀) → 共同的感情 → 同情
words.example: She expressed sympathy for the victims of the disaster.
words.example_zh: 她对灾难受害者表示了同情。

words.breakdown: em-(在…之中) + path-(感情) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the ability to understand and share the feelings of another
words.derivation: em(进入) + path(感情) + y(名词后缀) → 进入他人的感情 → 共情
words.example: A good leader must have empathy for their team members.
words.example_zh: 一个好的领导者必须对团队成员有同理心。

words.breakdown: a-(没有) + path-(感情) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern
words.derivation: a(没有) + path(感情) + y(名词后缀) → 没有感情 → 冷漠
words.example: Voter apathy is a growing concern in many democracies.
words.example_zh: 选民的冷漠是许多民主国家日益关注的问题。

words.meaning_en: the science of the causes and effects of diseases
words.derivation: path(疾病) + logy(学科) → 研究疾病的学科 → 病理学
words.example: She studied pathology in medical school.
words.example_zh: 她在医学院学习了病理学。

words.meaning_en: arousing pity, especially through vulnerability or sadness
words.derivation: path(感情) + etic(…的) → 引发感情的 → 令人同情的
words.example: The stray dog presented a pathetic sight in the rain.
words.example_zh: 流浪狗在雨中的样子令人心生怜悯。

words.meaning_en: a system for transmitting voices over a distance using wire or radio
words.derivation: tele(远) + phon(声音) → 传到远处的声音 → 电话
words.example: Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone.
words.example_zh: 亚历山大·格雷厄姆·贝尔发明了电话。

words.meaning_en: an instrument for converting sound waves into electrical signals
words.derivation: micro(微小) + phon(声音) → 捕捉微小声音的装置 → 麦克风
words.example: The speaker adjusted the microphone before the presentation.
words.example_zh: 演讲者在演讲前调整了麦克风。

words.meaning_en: relating to the sounds of speech
words.derivation: phon(声音) + etic(…的) → 与声音有关的 → 语音的
words.example: The dictionary includes phonetic transcriptions of each word.
words.example_zh: 这本词典包含每个单词的音标。

words.breakdown: sym-(共同) + phon-(声音) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: an elaborate musical composition for a full orchestra
words.derivation: sym(共同) + phon(声音) + y(名词后缀) → 声音和谐共鸣 → 交响乐
words.example: Beethoven composed nine symphonies during his lifetime.
words.example_zh: 贝多芬一生创作了九部交响乐。

words.meaning_en: the quality of being pleasing to the ear
words.derivation: eu(好) + phon(声音) + y(名词后缀) → 好听的声音 → 悦耳
words.example: The poet chose words for their euphony as well as their meaning.
words.example_zh: 诗人选词既注重含义也注重声音的悦耳。

words.meaning_en: a picture made using a camera
words.derivation: photo(光) + graph(记录) → 用光记录 → 照片
words.example: The photograph captured a beautiful sunset over the ocean.
words.example_zh: 这张照片捕捉到了海面上美丽的日落。

words.meaning_en: an account of someone's life written by someone else
words.derivation: bio(生命) + graph(写) + y(名词后缀) → 写下一生 → 传记
words.example: She wrote a biography of the famous scientist.
words.example_zh: 她写了一部关于这位著名科学家的传记。

words.breakdown: geo-(地球) + graph-(写，描述) + -y(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: the study of the physical features of the earth and its atmosphere
words.derivation: geo(地球) + graph(描述) + y(名词后缀) → 描述地球的学科 → 地理学
words.example: The geography of the region includes mountains and rivers.
words.example_zh: 该地区的地理特征包括山脉和河流。

words.meaning_en: a simplified drawing showing the structure of something
words.derivation: dia(通过) + gram(画) → 通过画来表达 → 图表
words.example: The professor drew a diagram to explain the process.
words.example_zh: 教授画了一张图表来解释这个过程。

words.meaning_en: a system for transmitting messages along a wire
words.derivation: tele(远) + graph(写) → 写到远方 → 电报
words.example: The telegraph revolutionized long-distance communication.
words.example_zh: 电报彻底改变了远距离通信方式。

words.meaning_en: the scientific study of living organisms
words.derivation: bio(生命) + logy(学科) → 研究生命的学科 → 生物学
words.example: She is majoring in biology at the university.
words.example_zh: 她在大学主修生物学。

words.meaning_en: the branch of biology dealing with organisms and their environment
words.derivation: eco(环境) + logy(学科) → 研究环境的学科 → 生态学
words.example: The professor teaches a course on marine ecology.
words.example_zh: 这位教授教授一门关于海洋生态学的课程。

words.meaning_en: the application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes
words.derivation: techno(技术) + logy(学科) → 关于技术的学科 → 科技
words.example: Technology has transformed the way we communicate.
words.example_zh: 科技改变了我们的交流方式。

words.meaning_en: a comparison between things that have similar features
words.derivation: ana(按照) + logy(推理) → 按照推理进行比较 → 类比
words.example: The teacher used an analogy to explain the complex concept.
words.example_zh: 老师用了一个类比来解释这个复杂的概念。

words.meaning_en: a conversation between two or more people
words.derivation: dia(之间) + log(语言) → 人与人之间的语言交流 → 对话
words.example: The novel contains realistic dialogue between its characters.
words.example_zh: 这部小说包含角色之间逼真的对话。

words.meaning_en: the variety of plant and animal life in the world or in a particular habitat
words.derivation: bio(生物) + diversity(多样性) → 生物的多样性 → 生物多样性
words.example: The rainforest is known for its incredible biodiversity.
words.example_zh: 热带雨林以其惊人的生物多样性而闻名。

words.meaning_en: an account of someone's life written by someone else
words.derivation: bio(生命) + graph(写) + y(名词后缀) → 写下一个人的一生 → 传记
words.example: He published a biography of Abraham Lincoln.
words.example_zh: 他出版了一部亚伯拉罕·林肯的传记。

words.meaning_en: the scientific study of living organisms
words.derivation: bio(生命) + logy(学科) → 研究生命的学科 → 生物学
words.example: Marine biology focuses on ocean life.
words.example_zh: 海洋生物学专注于海洋生命。

words.meaning_en: the branch of science concerned with chemical processes in living organisms
words.derivation: bio(生命) + chemistry(化学) → 生命体中的化学 → 生物化学
words.example: She earned her degree in biochemistry.
words.example_zh: 她获得了生物化学学位。

words.breakdown: anti-(反对) + bio-(生命) + -tic(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a medicine that inhibits the growth of or destroys microorganisms
words.derivation: anti(反对) + bio(生命) + tic(…的) → 反对微生物生命的 → 抗生素
words.example: The doctor prescribed an antibiotic for the bacterial infection.
words.example_zh: 医生为细菌感染开了抗生素。

words.meaning_en: persisting for a long time or constantly recurring
words.derivation: chron(时间) + ic(…的) → 持续很长时间的 → 慢性的
words.example: He suffers from chronic back pain.
words.example_zh: 他患有慢性背痛。

words.meaning_en: a factual written account of important events in the order of their occurrence
words.derivation: chron(时间) + icle(名词后缀) → 按时间记录的文献 → 编年史
words.example: The book is a chronicle of the nation's history.
words.example_zh: 这本书是一部国家历史的编年史。

words.breakdown: chron-(时间) + -o- + log-(学科) + -ical(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: arranged in the order of time
words.derivation: chron(时间) + o + log(学科) + ical(…的) → 按时间逻辑排列的 → 按时间顺序的
words.example: Please list the events in chronological order.
words.example_zh: 请按时间顺序列出这些事件。

words.breakdown: syn-(共同) + chron-(时间) + -ize(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to cause to occur or operate at the same time or rate
words.derivation: syn(共同) + chron(时间) + ize(使) → 使时间一致 → 使同步
words.example: The dancers synchronized their movements perfectly.
words.example_zh: 舞者们完美地同步了他们的动作。

words.breakdown: ana-(向后) + chron-(时间) + -ism(名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: a thing belonging to a period other than that in which it exists
words.derivation: ana(向后) + chron(时间) + ism(现象) → 时间倒错的现象 → 时代错误
words.example: Using a typewriter in the digital age seems like an anachronism.
words.example_zh: 在数字时代使用打字机似乎是一种时代错误。

words.meaning_en: to interrupt the normal course or unity of something
words.derivation: dis(分开) + rupt(断) → 打断使分开 → 扰乱
words.example: The protest disrupted traffic in the city center.
words.example_zh: 抗议活动扰乱了市中心的交通。

words.meaning_en: to burst out suddenly and violently
words.derivation: e(向外) + rupt(破) → 向外破裂 → 喷发
words.example: The volcano erupted without warning.
words.example_zh: 火山毫无预警地喷发了。

words.meaning_en: to stop the continuous progress of an activity or process
words.derivation: inter(在…之间) + rupt(断) → 在中间打断 → 中断
words.example: Please don't interrupt the speaker during the presentation.
words.example_zh: 请不要在演讲期间打断演讲者。

words.meaning_en: having or showing a willingness to act dishonestly for personal gain
words.derivation: cor(完全) + rupt(破) → 完全破坏(道德) → 腐败的
words.example: The corrupt officials were eventually brought to justice.
words.example_zh: 腐败官员最终被绳之以法。

words.meaning_en: sudden and unexpected
words.derivation: ab(离开) + rupt(断) → 突然断开 → 突然的
words.example: The meeting came to an abrupt end.
words.example_zh: 会议突然结束了。

words.meaning_en: able to be believed; convincing
words.derivation: cred(相信) + ible(能…的) → 能被相信的 → 可信的
words.example: The witness provided a credible account of the events.
words.example_zh: 证人对事件提供了可信的描述。

words.meaning_en: impossible to believe; extraordinary
words.derivation: in(不) + cred(相信) + ible(能…的) → 不能相信的 → 难以置信的
words.example: The speed of technological change is incredible.
words.example_zh: 技术变革的速度令人难以置信。

words.meaning_en: a qualification or achievement that proves capability
words.derivation: cred(信任) + ential(名词后缀) → 让人信任的凭证 → 资格证明
words.example: She presented her credentials to the hiring committee.
words.example_zh: 她向招聘委员会出示了自己的资格证明。

words.meaning_en: the ability to obtain goods before payment based on trust; recognition
words.derivation: cred(相信) + it → 基于相信给予的 → 信用
words.example: Students need 120 credits to graduate.
words.example_zh: 学生需要修满120个学分才能毕业。

words.meaning_en: to harm the good reputation of someone
words.derivation: dis(否定) + cred(相信) + it → 使人不再相信 → 使不可信
words.example: New evidence discredited the old theory.
words.example_zh: 新证据推翻了旧理论的可信度。

words.meaning_en: the body of words used in a particular language
words.derivation: voc(语言) + abul + ary(名词后缀) → 语言中可以叫出的词 → 词汇
words.example: Reading widely helps expand your vocabulary.
words.example_zh: 广泛阅读有助于扩大词汇量。

words.breakdown: ad-(朝向) + voc-(叫) + -ate(动词/名词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to publicly recommend or support; a person who supports a cause
words.derivation: ad(朝向) + voc(叫) + ate(做) → 朝着某方向呼喊 → 提倡
words.example: She advocates for environmental protection.
words.example_zh: 她倡导环境保护。

words.meaning_en: to stimulate or give rise to a reaction or emotion
words.derivation: pro(向前) + vok(叫) → 向前叫喊挑衅 → 激怒
words.example: The controversial speech provoked widespread debate.
words.example_zh: 这篇有争议的演讲引发了广泛的辩论。

words.meaning_en: to bring or recall to the conscious mind
words.derivation: e(向外) + vok(叫) → 把记忆叫出来 → 唤起
words.example: The music evoked memories of her childhood.
words.example_zh: 这首音乐唤起了她童年的回忆。

words.meaning_en: relating to the human voice; expressing opinions freely
words.derivation: voc(声音) + al(…的) → 与声音有关的 → 直言不讳的
words.example: She has been vocal about the need for educational reform.
words.example_zh: 她一直在公开呼吁教育改革的必要性。

words.meaning_en: to cause something to pass on from one place or person to another
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + mit(发送) → 跨越距离发送 → 传输
words.example: Radio waves transmit signals across long distances.
words.example_zh: 无线电波跨越远距离传输信号。

words.meaning_en: to present for consideration; to yield to authority
words.derivation: sub(在下方) + mit(送) → 从下方送上去 → 提交
words.example: Please submit your essay by Friday.
words.example_zh: 请在周五之前提交你的论文。

words.meaning_en: the production and discharge of something, especially gas or radiation
words.derivation: e(向外) + mis(送) + sion(行为) → 向外送出 → 排放
words.example: Carbon emissions contribute to global warming.
words.example_zh: 碳排放导致全球变暖。

words.meaning_en: to give authorization or consent to; an official document granting authorization
words.derivation: per(通过) + mit(送) → 让通过放行 → 允许
words.example: The city does not permit construction in this area.
words.example_zh: 该市不允许在这个区域进行施工。

words.meaning_en: an object forcibly propelled at a target
words.derivation: mis(发送) + sile(能…的) → 能被发射出去的 → 导弹
words.example: The military tested a new missile defense system.
words.example_zh: 军方测试了一种新型导弹防御系统。

words.meaning_en: a circumstance, fact, or influence that contributes to a result
words.derivation: fact(做) + or(…者) → 起作用的东西 → 因素
words.example: Cost is a major factor in the decision.
words.example_zh: 成本是决策中的一个重要因素。

words.breakdown: manu-(手) + fact-(制造) + -ure(名词/动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to make something on a large scale using machinery
words.derivation: manu(手) + fact(制造) + ure(行为) → 用手制造 → 生产
words.example: The company manufactures electronic devices.
words.example_zh: 这家公司制造电子设备。

words.meaning_en: a place provided for a particular purpose; ease of doing something
words.derivation: fac(做) + ility(名词后缀) → 方便做事的地方 → 设施
words.example: The university has excellent research facilities.
words.example_zh: 这所大学拥有出色的研究设施。

words.meaning_en: an object made by a human being, typically of historical interest
words.derivation: arti(技艺) + fact(制造) → 用技艺制造的东西 → 人工制品
words.example: The museum displays artifacts from ancient Egypt.
words.example_zh: 博物馆展出了来自古埃及的文物。

words.meaning_en: an inherent mental or physical power; the teaching staff of a university
words.derivation: fac(做) + ulty(名词后缀) → 有做事能力的人 → 全体教员
words.example: The faculty voted to change the curriculum.
words.example_zh: 全体教员投票决定修改课程。

words.breakdown: un-(不) + precedent-(先例) + -ed(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: never done or known before
words.derivation: un(不) + precedent(先例) + ed(…的) → 没有先例的 → 史无前例的
words.example: The country experienced unprecedented economic growth.
words.example_zh: 这个国家经历了史无前例的经济增长。

words.meaning_en: to estimate something to be smaller or less important than it actually is
words.derivation: under(低于) + estimate(估计) → 估计得过低 → 低估
words.example: Do not underestimate the difficulty of the exam.
words.example_zh: 不要低估这次考试的难度。

words.meaning_en: relating to or affecting all people in the world
words.derivation: uni(一) + vers(转) + al(…的) → 合为一体旋转的 → 普遍的
words.example: Access to clean water is a universal need.
words.example_zh: 获得清洁水源是全人类的普遍需求。

words.meaning_en: not likely to happen, be done, or be true
words.derivation: un(不) + likely(可能的) → 不可能的 → 不太可能的
words.example: It is unlikely that the project will finish on time.
words.example_zh: 这个项目不太可能按时完成。

words.meaning_en: to remove a cover from; to discover something hidden
words.derivation: un(去除) + cover(覆盖) → 去除覆盖 → 揭露
words.example: Archaeologists uncovered the remains of an ancient city.
words.example_zh: 考古学家发现了一座古城的遗迹。

words.meaning_en: to bring back to a former condition or position
words.derivation: re(回) + store(存放) → 放回原来的状态 → 恢复
words.example: The team worked to restore the damaged painting.
words.example_zh: 团队努力修复受损的画作。

words.meaning_en: to make previously unknown information known to others
words.derivation: re(回) + veal(面纱) → 揭开面纱 → 揭示
words.example: The study revealed a link between diet and disease.
words.example_zh: 这项研究揭示了饮食与疾病之间的关联。

words.meaning_en: to reexamine and make alterations to written or printed matter
words.derivation: re(再) + vis(看) → 再看一遍 → 修订
words.example: The author revised the manuscript several times.
words.example_zh: 作者多次修改了手稿。

words.meaning_en: capable of being renewed; not depleted when used
words.derivation: re(再) + new(新的) + able(能…的) → 能再次变新的 → 可再生的
words.example: Solar power is a renewable energy source.
words.example_zh: 太阳能是一种可再生能源。

words.meaning_en: to build or form something again after it has been damaged or destroyed
words.derivation: re(再) + construct(建造) → 再次建造 → 重建
words.example: Scientists are trying to reconstruct the ancient climate.
words.example_zh: 科学家正试图重建古代气候模型。

words.meaning_en: present as the strongest or main element
words.derivation: pre(在前) + dominant(支配的) → 在前面支配的 → 占主导地位的
words.example: English is the predominant language in international business.
words.example_zh: 英语是国际商务中的主导语言。

words.meaning_en: to maintain something in its original or existing state
words.derivation: pre(提前) + serve(保持) → 提前保持好 → 保存
words.example: We must preserve the natural environment for future generations.
words.example_zh: 我们必须为后代保护自然环境。

words.breakdown: pre-(在…之前) + limin-(门槛) + -ary(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: denoting an action or event preceding or done in preparation for something
words.derivation: pre(在…之前) + limin(门槛) + ary(…的) → 在门槛之前的 → 初步的
words.example: Preliminary results suggest the treatment is effective.
words.example_zh: 初步结果表明这种治疗方法是有效的。

words.breakdown: pre-(在…之前) + vi-(路) + -ous(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: existing or occurring before in time or order
words.derivation: pre(在…之前) + vi(路) + ous(…的) → 走在前面的 → 先前的
words.example: She had no previous experience in this field.
words.example_zh: 她在这个领域没有以往的经验。

words.meaning_en: to come before something in time, order, or position
words.derivation: pre(在…之前) + ced(走) → 走在前面 → 先于
words.example: A brief introduction will precede the main lecture.
words.example_zh: 正式讲座之前会有一个简短的介绍。

words.breakdown: dis-(分开) + stingu-(刺，标记) + -ish(动词后缀)
words.meaning_en: to recognize or treat someone or something as different
words.derivation: dis(分开) + stingu(标记) + ish(做) → 用标记分开 → 区分
words.example: It is important to distinguish between fact and opinion.
words.example_zh: 区分事实和观点是很重要的。

words.meaning_en: to give shares of something; to spread over an area
words.derivation: dis(分开) + tribut(给予) → 分开给予 → 分配
words.example: The organization distributes food to those in need.
words.example_zh: 该组织向有需要的人分发食物。

words.meaning_en: to get rid of; to arrange in a particular position
words.derivation: dis(离开) + pos(放置) + e → 放到一边去 → 处置
words.example: You should dispose of hazardous waste properly.
words.example_zh: 你应该妥善处理危险废物。

words.meaning_en: to make visible by putting in a noticeable position
words.derivation: dis(展开) + play(折叠) → 展开折叠的东西 → 展示
words.example: The museum displays artifacts from the colonial period.
words.example_zh: 博物馆展出了殖民时期的文物。

words.meaning_en: to make or become less
words.derivation: di(分开) + min(小) + ish(使) → 使变得更小 → 减少
words.example: The supply of fresh water is diminishing rapidly.
words.example_zh: 淡水供应正在迅速减少。

words.meaning_en: to make a thorough or dramatic change in form or appearance
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + form(形式) → 跨越原有形式 → 转变
words.example: Technology has transformed modern education.
words.example_zh: 科技改变了现代教育。

words.meaning_en: the process of changing from one state or condition to another
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + it(走) + ion(行为) → 走过去到另一边 → 过渡
words.example: The transition from high school to college can be challenging.
words.example_zh: 从高中到大学的过渡可能充满挑战。

words.breakdown: trans-(通过) + par-(出现) + -ent(形容词后缀)
words.meaning_en: allowing light to pass through; easy to perceive or detect
words.derivation: trans(通过) + par(出现) + ent(…的) → 能透过去看到的 → 透明的
words.example: The government should be transparent about its spending.
words.example_zh: 政府应该对其支出保持透明。

words.meaning_en: to move or transfer something to another place or situation
words.derivation: trans(跨越) + plant(种植) → 跨越位置重新种植 → 移植
words.example: The surgeon performed a heart transplant.
words.example_zh: 外科医生进行了心脏移植手术。

words.meaning_en: to go beyond the range or limits of something
words.derivation: trans(超越) + scend(攀登) → 攀登并超越 → 超越
words.example: Great art can transcend cultural boundaries.
words.example_zh: 伟大的艺术可以超越文化的界限。

meaning_en: noun: act, state, or result of
meaning_zh: 名词后缀：表示行为、状态或结果

words.breakdown: civil-(公民) + -ize(使成为) + -ation(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the stage of human social and cultural development considered most advanced
words.derivation: civil(公民) + ize(使成为) + ation(状态) → 使成为公民社会的状态 → 文明
words.example: Ancient Greek civilization made lasting contributions to philosophy.
words.example_zh: 古希腊文明对哲学做出了持久的贡献。

words.breakdown: con-(共同) + serv-(保持) + -ation(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the action of conserving something, especially the natural environment
words.derivation: con(共同) + serv(保持) + ation(行为) → 共同保持的行为 → 保护
words.example: Conservation of natural resources is essential for sustainability.
words.example_zh: 自然资源的保护对于可持续发展至关重要。

words.breakdown: migr-(迁移) + -ation(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the movement from one region or habitat to another
words.derivation: migr(迁移) + ation(行为) → 迁移的行为 → 迁移
words.example: Bird migration patterns are affected by climate change.
words.example_zh: 鸟类的迁徙模式受到气候变化的影响。

words.breakdown: ex-(向外) + pans-(展开) + -ion(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the action of becoming larger or more extensive
words.derivation: ex(向外) + pans(展开) + ion(行为) → 向外展开 → 扩张
words.example: The rapid expansion of the city caused environmental concerns.
words.example_zh: 城市的快速扩张引发了环境问题。

words.breakdown: e-(向外) + ros-(啃，侵蚀) + -ion(名词后缀：行为/状态)
words.meaning_en: the gradual destruction or diminution of something
words.derivation: e(向外) + ros(侵蚀) + ion(行为) → 向外啃蚀 → 侵蚀
words.example: Soil erosion is a major problem in deforested areas.
words.example_zh: 土壤侵蚀是森林被砍伐地区的一个主要问题。

meaning_en: adjective: full of, having the quality of
meaning_zh: 形容词后缀：充满…的，具有…性质的

words.breakdown: indi-(在…之内) + gen-(出生) + -ous(形容词后缀：具有…的)
words.meaning_en: originating or occurring naturally in a particular place
words.derivation: indi(在…之内) + gen(出生) + ous(…的) → 在当地出生的 → 本土的
words.example: The indigenous population has lived here for thousands of years.
words.example_zh: 原住民在这里生活了数千年。

words.breakdown: e-(超出) + norm-(标准) + -ous(形容词后缀：充满…的)
words.meaning_en: very large in size, quantity, or extent
words.derivation: e(超出) + norm(标准) + ous(…的) → 超出正常标准的 → 巨大的
words.example: The project requires an enormous amount of funding.
words.example_zh: 这个项目需要巨额资金。

words.breakdown: hazard(危险) + -ous(形容词后缀：充满…的)
words.meaning_en: risky; dangerous
words.derivation: hazard(危险) + ous(充满…的) → 充满危险的 → 有危险的
words.example: Hazardous chemicals must be stored carefully.
words.example_zh: 危险化学品必须小心存放。

words.breakdown: prosper(繁荣) + -ous(形容词后缀：充满…的)
words.meaning_en: successful in material terms; flourishing financially
words.derivation: prosper(繁荣) + ous(充满…的) → 充满繁荣的 → 繁荣的
words.example: The region became prosperous through trade.
words.example_zh: 该地区通过贸易变得繁荣昌盛。

words.breakdown: ambi-(两边) + gu-(驱动) + -ous(形容词后缀：具有…的)
words.meaning_en: open to more than one interpretation; unclear
words.derivation: ambi(两边) + gu(驱动) + ous(…的) → 被驱向两边的 → 模糊的
words.example: The instructions were ambiguous and confused the students.
words.example_zh: 说明含糊不清，让学生们感到困惑。

meaning_zh: 形容词后缀：能够…的，值得…的

words.meaning_en: able to be maintained at a certain rate or level
words.derivation: sustain(维持) + able(能…的) → 能够持续维持的 → 可持续的
words.example: We need to find sustainable sources of energy.
words.example_zh: 我们需要找到可持续的能源。

words.meaning_en: able to be reached or entered; easy to understand
words.derivation: access(接近) + ible(能…的) → 能够接近的 → 可接近的
words.example: The building is accessible to people with disabilities.
words.example_zh: 这座建筑对残障人士是无障碍的。

words.meaning_en: notably large in size, amount, or extent
words.derivation: consider(考虑) + able(值得…的) → 值得考虑的(规模) → 相当大的
words.example: The research required a considerable amount of time.
words.example_zh: 这项研究需要相当多的时间。

words.meaning_en: certain to happen; unavoidable
words.derivation: in(不) + evit(避免) + able(能…的) → 不能避免的 → 不可避免的
words.example: Change is inevitable in any growing organization.
words.example_zh: 在任何成长中的组织里，变化是不可避免的。

words.meaning_en: able to exist or occur together without conflict
words.derivation: com(共同) + pat(感受) + ible(能…的) → 能共同感受的 → 兼容的
words.example: The software is compatible with most operating systems.
words.example_zh: 这款软件与大多数操作系统兼容。

meaning_zh: 名词后缀：表示结果、行为或过程

words.breakdown: environ(围绕) + -ment(名词后缀：状态/结果)
words.meaning_en: the surroundings or conditions in which a person, animal, or plant lives
words.derivation: environ(围绕) + ment(状态) → 围绕在周围的状态 → 环境
words.example: Pollution is causing serious damage to the environment.
words.example_zh: 污染正在对环境造成严重损害。

words.meaning_en: a thing done successfully with effort, skill, or courage
words.derivation: achieve(实现) + ment(结果) → 实现的结果 → 成就
words.example: Landing on the moon was a remarkable achievement.
words.example_zh: 登上月球是一项非凡的成就。

words.breakdown: develop(发展) + -ment(名词后缀：过程/结果)
words.meaning_en: the process of developing or being developed
words.derivation: develop(发展) + ment(过程) → 发展的过程 → 发展
words.example: Economic development has improved living standards.
words.example_zh: 经济发展提高了生活水平。

words.breakdown: establish(建立) + -ment(名词后缀：结果/行为)
words.meaning_en: the action of establishing something; a business or organization
words.derivation: establish(建立) + ment(结果) → 建立的结果 → 机构
words.example: The establishment of the university dates back to 1850.
words.example_zh: 这所大学的建立可以追溯到1850年。

words.breakdown: assess(评估) + -ment(名词后缀：行为/结果)
words.meaning_en: the evaluation or estimation of the nature, quality, or ability of something
words.derivation: assess(评估) + ment(行为) → 评估的行为 → 评估
words.example: The teacher conducted a formal assessment of student progress.
words.example_zh: 老师对学生的进步进行了正式评估。

meaning_en: verb: to make, to become, to treat as

words.meaning_en: to send a group of settlers to establish political control over a place
words.derivation: colon(殖民地) + ize(使成为) → 使成为殖民地 → 殖民
words.example: European powers colonized many parts of the world.
words.example_zh: 欧洲列强殖民了世界许多地方。

words.meaning_en: to concentrate on and become expert in a particular area
words.derivation: special(特别的) + ize(使成为) → 使自己变得特别 → 专攻
words.example: She decided to specialize in environmental law.
words.example_zh: 她决定专攻环境法。

words.meaning_en: to reduce something to the smallest possible amount or degree
words.derivation: minim(最小) + ize(使成为) → 使成为最小 → 最小化
words.example: We should minimize waste to protect the environment.
words.example_zh: 我们应该减少浪费来保护环境。

words.meaning_en: to give special importance or prominence to something
words.derivation: em(在…中) + phas(显示) + ize(使) → 使之显现出来 → 强调
words.example: The professor emphasized the importance of critical thinking.
words.example_zh: 教授强调了批判性思维的重要性。

words.meaning_en: to describe the distinctive nature or features of something
words.derivation: character(特征) + ize(使成为) → 使具有特征 → 以…为特征
words.example: Dry summers and mild winters characterize the Mediterranean climate.
words.example_zh: 干燥的夏季和温和的冬季是地中海气候的特征。
